title: Pasania discocarpa
intro: Pasania discocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing fever, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Pasania discocarpa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pasania discocarpa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments to treat various skin conditions and wounds.

Pasania discocarpa is closely related to other species within the genus, which are known for their distinctive leaf shapes and aromatic properties.
